claim: FEWS NET and IPC classified over 23 million people in Ethiopia, Somalia, and Kenya as Crisis or worse food insecurity during the 2020-2023 drought, the worst in 40 years, with Somalia at risk of famine.

source.title: FEWS NET East Africa food security outlook

method: Reviewed FEWS NET reports, IPC classifications, and drought impact assessments.
limitations: IPC classifications rely on available survey data, which is sparse in conflict zones.
Food-insecurity magnitude estimates have substantial uncertainty.

claim: A 2021 systematic review found rainfall-based early warning systems predict food insecurity with moderate skill at 1-3 month leads but degrade rapidly beyond 6 months and fail to capture conflict-driven food crises.

source.title: Drought early warning systems for food security: a systematic review

method: Reviewed systematic search, inclusion criteria, meta-analysis results and skill assessment.
limitations: Review covers multiple regions and drought types.
Lead-time skill estimates depend on outcome definition and region.
